question: Alex has four books of different sizes which he wishes to place on a bookshelf. Unfortunately, the bookshelf has a conspicuous hole at one end through which only the smallest book can fall. If he wishes for all his books to stay stationary, in how many ways can he arrange his books?
A tangent from the point $(2 \cdot 1994, 2 \cdot 1994)$ to the circle $x^2 + y^2 = 1994^2$ touches circle at the point $(a,b).$  Compute $a + b.$
If $2^{x+1}=4^{x-7}$ and $8^{3y}=16^{-y+13}$, what is the value of $x+y$?

How many 3-letter words can we make from the letters A, B, C, and D, if we are allowed to repeat letters, and we must use the letter A at least once? (Here, a word is an arbitrary sequence of letters.)
